column2: In the context of diagnosing breast cancer lymph node metastasis through whole-slide imaging, visually descriptive features at both low and high resolutions include: at low resolution, metastatic lymph nodes typically appear as enlarged, irregularly shaped nodes with a loss of normal architectural organization and a mixed pattern of hyperchromatic, pleomorphic nuclei with indistinct or absent nuclear-cytoplasmic ratio, and increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios. At high resolution, distinct features such as the presence of tumor cells with coarse nucleoli, frequent mitotic figures, and areas of coagulative necrosis can be observed. Additionally, metastatic deposits often exhibit a "satellite" appearance with clusters of tumor cells surrounding the central area of necrosis, and may show evidence of stromal reaction with infiltration by inflammatory cells. These characteristics help in distinguishing metastatic lymph nodes from reactive or benign changes.

In the context of diagnosing breast cancer lymph node metastasis through whole-slide imaging, visually descriptive features at both low and high resolutions include: at low resolution, metastatic lymph nodes typically appear as enlarged, irregularly shaped nodes with a loss of normal architectural organization and a mixed or coarsely reticular pattern of fibrosis. At high resolution, characteristic features such as the presence of tumor cells with pleomorphism, nuclear atypia, and frequent mitotic figures can be observed. Additionally, metastatic deposits often exhibit a "satellite" appearance with clusters of tumor cells surrounding the central stroma, and may show areas of necrosis and hemorrhage. The capsule of the metastatic lymph node is often disrupted or infiltrated by tumor cells, and the lymphoid tissue within the node is replaced by tumor parenchyma.

In the context of diagnosing breast cancer lymph node metastasis through whole-slide imaging, visually descriptive features at both low and high resolutions include: at low resolution, metastatic lymph nodes typically appear as enlarged, irregularly shaped nodes with a loss of normal architectural organization and a mixed or coarsely reticular texture. At high resolution, distinct characteristics such as the presence of tumor cells with pleomorphism, nuclear atypia, and frequent mitotic figures can be observed. Additionally, metastatic deposits often exhibit a "satellite" pattern around the central area of the lymph node, with tumor cells infiltrating the paracortical and medullary regions, and may show areas of necrosis and hemorrhage. The stroma may appear more fibrotic and less vascular compared to normal lymph nodes, and immunohistochemical stains for specific markers like ER, PR, and HER2 can further confirm the origin of the metastatic lesion.

In the context of diagnosing breast cancer lymph node metastasis through whole-slide imaging, visually descriptive features at both low and high resolutions include: at low resolution, metastatic lymph nodes typically appear as enlarged, irregularly shaped nodes with a loss of normal architectural organization and a mixed pattern of hyperchromatic, pleomorphic nuclei with indistinct or absent nuclear-cytoplasmic ratio, and increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios. At high resolution, distinct features such as the presence of tumor cells with irregular nuclear shapes, nuclear pleomorphism, and nuclear atypia can be observed, along with evidence of tumor cell invasion into the lymph node stroma, often accompanied by a reactive stromal response characterized by increased vascularity and inflammation. Additionally, metastatic deposits may show areas of coagulative necrosis and the presence of clear, eosinophilic, or mucinous material indicative of tumor burden.

In the context of diagnosing breast cancer lymph node metastasis through whole-slide imaging, visually descriptive features at both low and high resolutions include: at low resolution, metastatic lymph nodes typically appear as enlarged, irregularly shaped nodes with a loss of normal architectural organization and a mixed or coarsely reticular pattern of fibrosis. At high resolution, characteristic features such as the presence of tumor cells with pleomorphism, nuclear atypia, and frequent mitotic figures can be observed. Additionally, metastatic deposits often exhibit a perinuclear halo of clear space around tumor cells, which is less common in primary breast tissue.
